etymology.description: 直 ("looking forward") + 心 ("heart, mind") -> semantic components forming the original character
etymology.interpretation: The character 𢛳 originally combines 直 (zhí, "looking forward") indicating steadfastness and 心 (xīn, "heart, mind") representing the location of virtue and conscience, emphasizing a forward-looking mindset or virtue stemming from the heart. It served as an intermediate form for 德 (dé, "virtue") before evolving into the standard character.
